testcase.name: testInvalidLogin

failure.message: Error message should appear expected [false] but found [true]

testcase.name: testSearch

failure.message: invalid selector: Unable to locate an element with the xpath expression //input[@placeholder*='Search'] because of the following error: SyntaxError: Failed to execute 'evaluate' on 'Document': The string '//input[@placeholder*='Search']' is not a valid XPath expression. (Session info: chrome=148.0.7778.217) For documentation on this error, please visit: https://www.selenium.dev/documentation/webdriver/troubleshooting/errors#invalid-selector-exception Build info: version: '4.18.1', revision: 'b1d3319b48' System info: os.name: 'Windows 11', os.arch: 'amd64', os.version: '10.0', java.version: '17.0.17' Driver info: org.openqa.selenium.chrome.ChromeDriver Command: [dafeec5b786ce5eb6a7ee738332b643d, findElement {value=//input[@placeholder*='Search'], using=xpath}] Capabilities {acceptInsecureCerts: false, browserName: chrome, browserVersion: 148.0.7778.217, chrome: {chromedriverVersion: 148.0.7778.178 (d096af1c9e9..., userDataDir: C:\Users\ASUS\AppData\Local...}, fedcm:accounts: true, goog:chromeOptions: {debuggerAddress: localhost:50585}, goog:processID: 22844, networkConnectionEnabled: false, pageLoadStrategy: normal, platformName: windows, proxy: Proxy(), se:cdp: ws://localhost:50585/devtoo..., se:cdpVersion: 148.0.7778.217, setWindowRect: true, strictFileInteractability: false, timeouts: {implicit: 0, pageLoad: 300000, script: 30000}, unhandledPromptBehavior: dismiss and notify, webauthn:extension:credBlob: true, webauthn:extension:largeBlob: true, webauthn:extension:minPinLength: true, webauthn:extension:prf: true, webauthn:virtualAuthenticators: true} Session ID: dafeec5b786ce5eb6a7ee738332b643d

You may need to include a dependency on a specific version of the CDP using something similar to `org.seleniumhq.selenium:selenium-devtools-v86:4.18.1` where the version ("v86") matches the version of the chromium-based browser you're using and the version number of the artifact is the same as Selenium's.

testcase.name: testProductDetailNavigation

testcase.name: validLoginTest

failure.message: Login failed - not redirected to home expected [true] but found [false]
